observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Pending the implementation of a computerized accounting system, require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for Cash in Bank-Local Currency Current Account;
Thereafter, prepare appropriate BRS for all bank accounts of the Municipality by reconciling the bank statements and Cashbooks with the subsidiary ledgers for Cash in Bank-Local Currency Current Account; and
Augment personnel in the Accounting Unit to prioritize the preparation and submission of the required BRS.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Assessor-In-Charge of Office to provide copies of tax declarations for land properties to the Municipal Accountant, as basis for the latter to establish a subsidiary list and as basis to elect the revaluation model in accounting for land as prescribed under IPSAS 17 using latest available Fair Market Value; and
Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to provide appropriate disclosures in the financial statements in accordance with the disclosure requirements of IPSAS 17.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Enjoin the Inventory Committee to:
Conduct physical inventory count at least once a year and submit to the Audit Team the corresponding Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and Report on Physical Count of Local Road Networks for local roads; and
Expedite the One-Time Cleansing of the PPE account in accordance with the procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006, to ensure that the PPE balances are fairly presented in the financial statements.
Provide additional manpower at the Accounting Office and GSO to aide in the preparation of records/reports and in the supply and property management of the LGU;
Require the GSO and Municipal Accountant to reconcile PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards regularly.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Engineer to furnish the Status Report of the projects still recorded under the Construction in Progress account as presented above including the Certificates of Completion, if any to the Municipal Accountant as basis for appropriate adjustment in the books;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw adjusting entries to reclassify the cost of the completed project Construction of Public Market from CIP to Investment Property, Buildings and recognize the related depreciation expense; and
For the projects recorded under the General Fund with no available supporting documents, effect the dropping in the books by drawing appropriate adjusting entries.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the 125 DVs with complete supporting documents and to adhere to the prescribed timeline in the submission of paid vouchers and documents to the Auditor concerned pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006; and
Instruct the LDRRM Council to incorporate the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in its LDRRMF Investment Plan.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant deduct PhilHealth premiums from the salaries of the subject officials and employees and remit the same to PhilHealth together with the government share.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that all Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) charged under the 20% Development Fund are aligned with the approved AIP and contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Local Government Unit (LGU) in accordance with DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2020-1.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Human Resource Management Officer to:
Develop a standardized JO contract instead of memorandum order that outlines, among others, the duration of employment, terms and conditions, specific duties and responsibilities, office assignments, rates, and work schedules;
Review and update the duties and responsibilities in the contracts to ensure clarity and relevance of assigned tasks; and
Formulate policy guidelines that will set the rates to be paid to job order employees in accordance with existing rules and regulations in hiring of JO.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Strengthen the gender-responsive monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system by updating the composition of the GAD M&E Team;
Subsequently, conduct regular assessment of institutional mechanisms for gender mainstreaming to monitor the effectiveness and efficiency of GAD implementation; and
Prepare and ensure the timely submission of the annual LGU Status Report on Institutional Mechanisms for Gender Mainstreaming to the DILG Regional Office.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Pending the implementation of a computerized accounting system, require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for Cash in Bank-Local Currency Current Account;

Thereafter, prepare appropriate BRS for all bank accounts of the Municipality by reconciling the bank statements and Cashbooks with the subsidiary ledgers for Cash in Bank-Local Currency Current Account; and

Augment personnel in the Accounting Unit to prioritize the preparation and submission of the required BRS.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the Municipal Assessor-In-Charge of Office to provide copies of tax declarations for land properties to the Municipal Accountant, as basis for the latter to establish a subsidiary list and as basis to elect the revaluation model in accounting for land as prescribed under IPSAS 17 using latest available Fair Market Value; and

Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to provide appropriate disclosures in the financial statements in accordance with the disclosure requirements of IPSAS 17.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Enjoin the Inventory Committee to:

Conduct physical inventory count at least once a year and submit to the Audit Team the corresponding Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and Report on Physical Count of Local Road Networks for local roads; and

Expedite the One-Time Cleansing of the PPE account in accordance with the procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006, to ensure that the PPE balances are fairly presented in the financial statements.

Provide additional manpower at the Accounting Office and GSO to aide in the preparation of records/reports and in the supply and property management of the LGU;

Require the GSO and Municipal Accountant to reconcile PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards regularly.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Require the Municipal Engineer to furnish the Status Report of the projects still recorded under the Construction in Progress account as presented above including the Certificates of Completion, if any to the Municipal Accountant as basis for appropriate adjustment in the books; and

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw adjusting entries to reclassify the cost of the completed project Construction of Public Market from CIP to Investment Property, Buildings and recognize the related depreciation expense.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 

Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the 125 DVs with complete supporting documents and to adhere to the prescribed timeline in the submission of paid vouchers and documents to the Auditor concerned pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006; and 

Instruct the LDRRM Council to incorporate the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in its LDRRMF Investment Plan.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant deduct PhilHealth premiums from the salaries of the subject officials and employees and remit the same to PhilHealth together with the government share.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that all Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) charged under the 20% Development Fund are aligned with the approved AIP and contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Local Government Unit (LGU) in accordance with DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2020-1.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Human Resource Management Officer to:

Develop a standardized JO contract instead of memorandum order that outlines, among others, the duration of employment, terms and conditions, specific duties and responsibilities, office assignments, rates, and work schedules;

Review and update the duties and responsibilities in the contracts to ensure clarity and relevance of assigned tasks; and

Formulate policy guidelines that will set the rates to be paid to job order employees in accordance with existing rules and regulations in hiring of JO.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Strengthen the gender-responsive monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system by updating the composition of the GAD M&E Team;

Subsequently, conduct regular assessment of institutional mechanisms for gender mainstreaming to monitor the effectiveness and efficiency of GAD implementation; and

Prepare and ensure the timely submission of the annual LGU Status Report on Institutional Mechanisms for Gender Mainstreaming to the DILG Regional Office.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

Update the RPTARs/RPTICs and prepare a duly certified list of taxpayers with corresponding amount of tax due and collectible for the year which shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in the set-up of RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year; and

Prepare a complete list of Delinquent Taxpayers and post in appropriate public places as required  under Section 254 of R.A. 7160.

We recommended that management:

If projects are already completed and no outstanding obligations accrue to the funds, return immediately the unexpended balance to the source agencies.  If the SA cannot be identified, remit the remaining idle funds to the Bureau of Treasury pursuant to DOF, DBM and COA Joint Circular No. 4-2012 dated September 11, 2012 which prescribes the rules and regulations implementing Executive Order (E.O.) No. 431 dated May 20, 2005 directing the reversion of all dormant accounts, unnecessary special and trust funds to the general fund of the national treasury.

We recommended that management enforce control activities, specifically by seeking assistance from the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan, and the Municipal Engineer for possible amendment on the revenue ordinance to comply with ACEL Rates.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountxd ant to reconcile the bank balance of DRRM-TF with the balances per Cash in Bank, LCCA SL, Treasurer’s Cashbook and Trust Liabilities-DRRM account and effect necessary adjusting entries.

For more effective administration of the said offices which in turn will benefit the Municipality as a whole, we recommended that Management publish, post in three conspicuous places and fill in the vacant mandatory position of Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, as prescribed in Sections 443 and 476 of RA 7160 and in accordance with existing guidelines, rules and regulations of the Civil Service Commission.

Streamline and document its donation policy and process thru the issuance of an Executive Order or a Memorandum, which among others, would contain the employees authorized to receive donations and their reportorial responsibilities.

observations.managementAction: For CY 2023 a record of check issuance per bank account is prepared by the Accounting Unit, however deposits were not included in the said records making it not useful for purposes of preparing the BRS.
observations.reason: Still no additional manpower is assigned at the Accounting Unit.

observations.managementAction: The municipality is still in the process of taking the inventory of all properties to identify the existing serviceable and unserviceable items. The LGU already required all departments to conduct inventory of existing properties in their respective offices and consequently identify the serviceable and unserviceable items.    As recommended, the municipality will create an Inventory Committee to conduct the one-time cleansing of all Property, Plant and Equipment.
observations.reason: Lack of enough manpower, absence of prior years’ records and difficulty in implementing all the required procedures on the one-time cleansing of PPE.

observations.recommendation:
Require the GSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant to obtain from the donor the fair value of the donated two (2) units Rice Reapers, as basis in the recognition of the assets in the books of the LGU; and

Ensure that donated assets, if any, for the ensuing periods with the Municipality as Donee are properly recognized in the books with due consideration of the terms and conditions embodied in the Memorandum of Agreement and/or Deed of Donation.
observations.managementAction: Only the CCTV set was recognized in the books.  MENRO still requested documents from the donor agency, for the fair value of 2 units rice reapers as basis for booking of the donated assets.  The donated assets will be recognized in the books as soon as the LGU receive     the documents with corresponding fair value.
observations.reason: No documents were received from the donor containing the fair value of 2 units rice reapers.

observations.recommendation:
Publish, post in three (3) conspicuous places and fill in the vacant mandatory positions, as prescribed in Sections 443, 472 and 476 of RA 7160 and in accordance with existing guidelines, rules and regulations of the Civil Service Commission; and

Provide appropriate funding for personal services for the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator position.
observations.managementAction: The two vacant mandatory positions were already published in the CSC Bulletin of Vacant Positions in the Government in the CSC website and were posted in three (3) conspicuous places in the municipality, however the said positions were still unfilled as of December 31, 2023.
observations.reason: No applications were received by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: Streamline and document its donation policy and process thru the issuance of an Executive Order or a Memorandum, which among others, would contain the employees authorized to receive donations and their reportorial responsibilities.
observations.managementAction: The Management adopted its donation policy, however said policy does not specify the employees authorized to receive donations and their reportorial responsibilities.

observations.recommendation: Establish specific guidelines on the grants of AICS and other financial assistance to indigent constituents which shall be in line with DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 11, series of 2019: Revised Guidelines on the Implementation of Assistance to Individuals in Crisis Situation to ensure equitable distribution of financial assistance among the qualified beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Management adopted its guidelines on the grant of AICS, however, said guidelines contain no ceiling or limit of amount for each kind of assistance

observations.recommendation: Prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Subsidiary Ledgers and Schedules of GL accounts to substantiate the validity and correctness of the accounts in the financial statements, pursuant to the above-mentioned provision.
observations.managementAction: SLs were prepared but not for all accounts (i.e. still no SLs were pared for Cash in Bank accounts)
observations.reason: Due to volume of transactions processed/recorded by the Accounting Unit, SLs were not completely prepared for all accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:
Enjoin the LAOO I/In-Charge of Office to fast track the updating of Assessment Roll and provide a copy to the Municipal Treasurer as basis for updating the RPTARs/RPTICs;
Thereafter direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Update the RPTARs/RPTICs and prepare a duly certified list of taxpayers with corresponding amount of tax due and collectible for the year which shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in the set-up of RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year; and
Prepare a complete list of Delinquent Taxpayers and post in appropriate public places as required under Section 254 of R.A. 7160.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to evaluate the schedule of Due to/Due from Other Funds accounts; determine which items need to be reclassified and prepare adjusting journal entries as appropriate.  Henceforth, ensure that these reciprocal accounts are reconciled for fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to present the correction of prior period errors/adjustments by restating the comparative amounts for the period(s) presented in which adjustments/errors occurred, whenever practicable; and
Provide adequate disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements to achieve fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:
Increase the fidelity bonds of the Collecting Officers in compliance with Section 7.2 of TC No. 02-2019 dated April 25, 2019 and Section 4.1.1.1 of Treasury Circular No. 1-2022 dated May 30, 2022; and
Require the Collecting Officers to determine the highest amount collected in a given day in the preceding year for the computation of appropriate amount of bond premium during renewal of fidelity bond.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with the requirement of RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 by establishing procedures which will ensure that cash pertaining to the unexpended balance of LDRRMF is fully transferred to the Trust Fund at year-end.

We recommended that management enforce control activities, specifically by:
Seeking assistance from the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan, and the Municipal Engineer for possible amendment on the revenue ordinance to comply with ACEL Rates; and
Requiring all persons responsible in the heavy equipment rental process to strictly observe the control activities as presented in the foregoing table to ensure that the objective of maximizing income generation is achieved by the LGU.

We recommend that management require the GAD Focal Point System to submit a copy of the DILG approved annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report to the COA Audit team assigned to the agency within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer was relieved from her designation as OIC-Municipal Assessor, however, the mandatory position of Municipal Assessor was still unfilled.
observations.reason: The vacancy was already posted but no application was received by the LGU

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the necessary adjusting entries to effect the dropping of the items costing below ₱50,000.00 in the PPE account. Subsequent purchases of equipment and other items with acquisition cost of less than P50,000.00 shall be recorded as expense once issued with the corresponding Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) to establish accountability over the assets; and 

Require the Supply Officer to prepare and maintain the required forms, registries and reports mentioned in Section 4.7 of COA Circular No. 2022-004 to strengthen control and safeguards over semi-expendable properties.
observations.managementAction:
JEV was prepared. Subsequent purchases of items with cost of less than P50,000.00 is recorded as expense and corresponding ICS is issued.




Forms/registries/reports were prepared for new purchases
Transfer of unexpended balance from CYs 2019-2022 was made to the Trust Fund and SLs by year of transfer was maintained.
observations.reason:
Incomplete Bank Statements



Preparation of the report was not prioritized

observations.recommendation:
Require the LDRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant to review the Report of Utilization of DRRM funds and afterwards prepare a revised report which shall include the complete sources of funds to determine total funds available for proper planning and decision-making pertaining to the utilization of the said funds; and

Direct the LDRRM Office to prepare and maintain a monitoring on the sources and utilization of LDRRM funds and reconcile the same with records of the Accounting and/or Budget Office in order to come up with a reliable Utilization Report.
observations.managementAction: The recommendations were implemented by the management.

observations.recommendation:
Enjoin the Municipal Treasurer to comply with the provision as mandated under Section 254 of the Local Government Code for the posting requirement of the Notice of Delinquency as a condition on availing judicial or administrative remedies which the Provincial Treasury Office may pursue; and 

Considering the huge amount of delinquent accounts, conduct extensive tax campaigns and develop other feasible strategies to enhance collection of delinquent accounts from taxpayers.
observations.managementAction: The recommendations were implemented by the management.

observations.recommendation: Refrain from conducting LGU-led trainings, planning conferences and other similar activities outside the locality unless extremely necessary, and exercise prudence in such a way that government funds are protected and conserved, and unnecessary expenditures are prevented.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to require the approved DTRs before processing payments for RATA as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001; and

Stop the grant of RATA to the LDRRM Officer II who fell short of the requirements for the RATA entitlement and the OIC-MPDC whose designation contravenes Section 13(c.6) of Rule IV of the ORAOHRA.
observations.managementAction: The recommendations were fully implemented by the management.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Development Council to determine specific programs, projects, and activities to be funded out of the 20% DF and ensure that these are well-planned, procurement and implementation-ready in accordance with Item 3.2.3 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020; and

Identify specific national government programs and projects to be provided with LGU counterpart in case an Infrastructure Support Fund (counterpart for national government project) will still be established.
observations.managementAction: The recommendations were fully implemented by the management.
The recommendations were fully implemented by the management.

observations.recommendation: Strictly comply with the provisions of EO 77 and stop the practice of paying travel expenses more than the amount authorized under the aforementioned EO.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation was fully implemented by the management.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Subsidiary Ledgers and Schedules of GL accounts to substantiate the validity and correctness of the accounts in the financial statements, pursuant to the above-mentioned provision.
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the above-mentioned provision and effect the necessary adjusting journal entry to recognize the asset account and depreciation.

We have recommended the management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the PPE in accordance with the criteria set under Item 4.2 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 and prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to make necessary adjusting entries to effect the dropping of the PPE costing below P15,000.00 and provide a copy of the JEV to the GSO together with the schedule or list of affected PPE for their subsequent dropping from the property/equipment records.

We have recommended the municipal accountant to reclassify the current and non-current portion of the long-term loans payable to Loans Payable – Current, Domestic account and Loans Payable, Long-Term Domestic, respectively and present these payables under the appropriate category in the financial statements.

We have  recommended that the insurance premium covering the whole year be taken up as Prepaid Insurance and only the portion of the prepayment that has been used up be recognized as Insurance Expense. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant draw an adjusting entry for the reclassification.

We have recommended that management maximize the utilization of the unexpended balance from prior years of the 5% LDRRM fund to strengthen preparations for disaster risk reduction and management in compliance with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013, and ensure the amounts reflected in the submitted reports are comparable to streamline verification.

We have recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant:
To recognize as asset all PPEs with major improvements, additions, alterations, replacements, repairs when these extend the life, increase the capacity or improve the efficiency or safety of the property, and
To observe the above-mentioned provision and effect the necessary adjusting journal entry to recognize the asset account and depreciation expense relative to these assets during the period.

observations.managementAction: All procurements already undergo proper bidding.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the management to secure a copy of masterlist as basis for validation to capture the profile of prospective families as beneficiaries; and

Direct the validation team to timely submit to the audit team a report/status of their verification.

We have recommended that management instruct the MDRRMO thru coordination with concerned municipal officials to submit to the OCD and the COA a complete list of 
donations received and distributed by the municipality.

We have recommended the management to secure a copy of masterlist as basis for validation to capture the profile of prospective families as beneficiaries; and
Direct the validation team to timely submit to the audit team a report/status of their verification.

We have recommended that management instruct the MDRRMO thru coordination with concerned municipal officials to submit to the OCD and the COA a complete list of donations received and distributed by the municipality.

observations.managementAction: Granting of cash advances to elected officials has been totally stoped.
All procurements already undergo proper bidding.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to management the following:

1. Require the HRMO in-charge to act on and/or process a complete retirement application within the period provided, and

2. Strictly comply with the provisions of RA 10154 and its IRR and give the highest priority to the payment and/or settlement of the retirees pensions, gratuities and/or other retirement benefits once all the requirements are complied with by the retiring employee.
observations.managementAction: Terminal benefits has already been fully paid. The management will be allocating annually terminal benefits starting from 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to management the following:

1.To instruct the property officer to maintain a record of property card for each item of PPE; 

2.To conduct physical count of all PPEs owned by the agency, through its duly designated members of the Inventory Committee, at least once a year; and

3.To reconcile accounts between the  Accounting Unit and the Property Officer from time to time.
observations.managementAction: Schedules on properties and Equipments are intact on the current years. The problem lies on the schedule of properties prior to 1998 which the management finds difficult to trace the properties due to improper turn over and missing records.

Partially completed (except the expansion of aloran water supply system phase III) in the sense that the primary line of electricity is not yet completely installed and the transmission line along tha national highway was affected due to road widening.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management submit to COA copies of the Memorandum of Agreement on aforementioned fund transfers and effect the submission thereon whenever future fund transfers are involved.
observations.managementAction: Partially submitted copies of MOA  to COA since the other copies are still in the regional office especially DILG funded projects.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to require the IBAMO, Inc. to submit the lacking documentary requirements prescribed under COA Circular No. 2007-001.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to management the following:
to instruct the property officer to maintain a record of property card for each item of PPE;
to conduct physical count of all PPEs owned by the agency, through its duly designated members of the Inventory Committee, at least once a year; and
to reconcile accounts between the  Accounting Unit and the Property Officer from time to time.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended to management the following:
Require the HRMO in-charge to act on and/or process a complete retirement application within the period provided, and
Strictly comply with the provisions of RA 10154 and its IRR and give the highest priority to the payment and/or settlement of the retirees pensions, gratuities and/or other retirement benefits once all the requirements are complied with by the retiring employee.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following:
Justify why these various disbursements were granted;
Stop the practice of granting cash advances to elected official unless for their official traveling expenses only; and
Faithful adherence to the above-cited provision is hereby enjoined, otherwise, disallowed in audit.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that management submit to COA copies of the Memorandum of Agreement on aforementioned fund transfers and effect the submission thereon whenever future fund transfers are involved.

observations.managementAction:
Usage of gasoline to private vehicles were loaded only during emergency cases (bantay-dagat for illegal fishing) and already stopped the practice.

Stickers already requested and purchased.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

Direct the HRMO to execute contract for employees hired under JO to include statement of duties and responsibilities as a means by which their performance can be measured. 

Submit the contract of services/job orders and Accomplishment Report for every paid payroll.

Strictly observe Section 4 (6) of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001 on government documentary requirements.
observations.managementAction: Executed JO contract and attached statement of duties and responsibilities in every paid payroll.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to management that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the appropriate accounts.  It is further recommended that future transactions be analyzed and classified as to the prescribed accounts in COA Circular No. 2015-009 in order to avoid misstatements and to fairly present the balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Necessary adjusting entries have been made by the accounting office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to management to instruct the Management Accountant to prioritize the remittance of the Real Property Taxes share to the barangays to enable them to use the said shares for projects and programs that are of great use to the barangays and its constituents, and allowing barangay officials to improve and strengthen their capabilities in delivering quality basic services.

We have recommended to Management that complete physical inventory-taking of the properties should be undertaken and Report on the Physical Count of Property Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) be prepared and submitted; require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to maintain Property Cards, Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for each  category of assets, and Real Property Ledger Cards for land; and require them further to conduct periodic reconciliation of the ledger card balances in order to achieve accuracy and fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.reason: Inventory created but actual physical count on inventories partially performed.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to management to require the Philippine Councilors’ League Incorporated and League of Municipalities of the Philippines to issue a government official receipt for all collections made to establish proper financial accountability.
observations.reason: Communicated with the PCL, however PCL still cannot provide Government OR as of date.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation/s:
We recommended that management:
Direct the HRMO to execute contract for employees hired under JO to include statement of duties and responsibilities as a means by which their performance can be measured.
Submit the contract of services/job orders and Accomplishment Report for every paid payroll.
Strictly observe Section 4 (6) of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001 on government documentary requirements.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to management that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the appropriate accounts.  It is further recommended that future transactions be analyzed and classified as to the prescribed accounts in COA Circular No. 2015-009 in order to avoid misstatements and to fairly present the balances in the financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended to management to instruct the Management Accountant to prioritize the remittance of the Real Property Taxes share to the barangays to enable them to use the said shares for projects and programs that are of great use to the barangays and its constituents, and allowing barangay officials to improve and strengthen their capabilities in delivering quality basic services.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to require the IBAMO, Inc. to submit the lacking documentary requirements prescribed under COA Circular No. 2007-001.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to Management that complete physical inventory-taking of the properties should be undertaken and Report on the Physical Count of Property Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) be prepared and submitted; require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to maintain Property Cards, Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for each  category of assets, and Real Property Ledger Cards for land; and require them further to conduct periodic reconciliation of the ledger card balances in order to achieve accuracy and fair presentation of the financial statements.

We have recommended to Management that funds set aside intended for programs, projects and activities for children’s protection equivalent to 1% allocation of IRA be implemented and expended strictly for the purpose.

We have recommended to Management the following:

1)	Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the MEEDO Officer to segregate in their reports for each special or economic enterprise account income and operating costs for easy determination of net income; and

2)	Instruct the Municipal Accountant to segregate each special account according to its distinctiveness and reflect the actual operations in each of the special accounts affected pursuant to Section 106 of COA Circular No. 2002-003.

3)	Require the LEE Officer to prepare and submit reports as enumerated in 6.6.2 of the Manual on the Setting Up and Operation of Local Economic Enterprises as part of the physical and financial reports of the LGU

We have recommended to Management the following:

 That the Municipal Engineer should carefully examine the Program of Work to avoid omission of very important scope of work to make the project operational or to avoid request for future variation orders, extra works so as to avoid delays in the implementation of infrastructure projects; 

 The Municipal Planning Development Officer should look into the duly approved program of works especially on the completeness of the needed scope of works and its attached extra work orders to ensure that the same is within its AIP and that the delivery of its basic services to its constituents/end users be rightfully served on time; and

3.	That the Municipal Budget Officer should specifically appropriate or allot a budget for the Operations and Maintenance (O & M) of InfRES subprojects from their 20% Development Fund Budget yearly for sustainability of said projects as LGU-Aloran counterpart.
observations.reason: Management have procured the transformer and pump for use in the completion of Rural Water System, Infres Project located in Brgy. Mitazan.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to Management that complete physical inventory-taking of the properties should be undertaken and Report on the Physical Count of Property Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) be prepared and submitted; require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to maintain Property Cards, Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for each  category of assets, and Real Property Ledger Cards for land; and require them further to conduct periodic reconciliation of the ledger card balances in order to achieve accuracy and fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.reason: Conducted inventory taking and still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to Management to require the Municipal Accountant to adhere PPSAS No. 17 in order to achieve accuracy and fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.reason: Updated  current record of depreciation except those acquired prior years.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to Management that:

Actual physical inventory-taking of the properties should be undertaken 	and Report on the Physical Count of Property Plant and Equipment RPCPPE) be prepared and submitted;

Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to maintain Property Cards, Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for each 	category of assets, and Real Property Ledger Cards for land; and

Require them further to conduct periodic reconciliation of the ledger card balances in order to achieve accuracy and fair presentation of the financial statements.

observations.recommendation:

We have recommended to management to require the Philippine Councilors’ League Incorporated and League of Municipalities of the Philippines to issue a government official receipt for all collections made to establish proper financial accountability.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to Management that funds set aside intended for programs, projects and activities for children’s protection equivalent to 1% allocation of IRA be implemented and expended strictly for the purpose.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to Management the following:
1) 	Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the MEEDO Officer to segregate in their reports for each special or economic enterprise account income and operating costs for easy determination of net income;
2) 	Instruct the Municipal Accountant to segregate each special account according to its distinctiveness and reflect the actual operations in each of the special accounts affected pursuant to Section 106 of COA Circular No. 2002-003; and
3) 	Require the LEE Officer to prepare and submit reports as enumerated in 6.6.2 of the Manual on the Setting Up and Operation of Local Economic Enterprises as part of the physical and financial reports of the LGU

Recommendation:
We have recommended to Management the following:
That the Municipal Engineer should carefully examine the Program of Work to avoid omission of very important scope of work to make the project operational or to avoid request for future variation orders, extra works so as to avoid delays in the implementation of infrastructure projects;
The Municipal Planning Development Officer should look into the duly approved program of works especially on the completeness of the needed scope of works and its attached extra work orders to ensure that the same is within its AIP and that the delivery of its basic services to its constituents/end users be rightfully served on time; and
That the Municipal Budget Officer should specifically appropriate or allot a budget for the Operations and Maintenance (O & M) of InfRES subprojects from their 20% Development Fund Budget yearly for sustainability of said projects as LGU-Aloran counterpart.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to Management that complete physical inventory-taking of the properties should be undertaken and Report on the Physical Count of Property Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) be prepared and submitted; require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to maintain Property Cards, Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for each  category of assets, and Real Property Ledger Cards for land; and require them further to conduct periodic reconciliation of the ledger card balances in order to achieve accuracy and fair presentation of the financial statements.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to Management to require the Municipal Accountant to adhere PPSAS No. 17 in order to achieve accuracy and fair presentation of the financial statements.

We have recommended to Management that contracts/Purchase Orders should not be entered into without the back-up appropriation. Thus, the LGU should first present the Purchase Request to the Municipal Budget Officer for obligation before the procurement shall be undertaken; claims relating to the budget year shall be obligated out of that year’s budget. Moreover, the Municipality should spend within its budget and should avoid incurring expenses beyond the limits; and the Municipal Treasurer should not certify as to the availability of funds if no funds were really available for the purpose.
We have recommended to management to require the Municipal Accountant to adhere PPSAS No. 17 in order to achieve accuracy and fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.reason: PPE – not easily identified in the previous years’ acquisition.

Officer for obligation before the procurement shall be undertaken; claims
relating to the budget year shall be obligated out of that year’s budget.
Moreover, the Municipality should spend within its budget and should avoid

of the properties should be undertaken and Report on the Physical Count of
Property Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) be prepared and submitted; require
the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to maintain Property
Cards,Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for each category of assets,

a. Direct the Municipality Accountant to give utmost priority in the preparation of monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements.
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to closely supervise personnel tasked to prepare the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Reports.
c. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to conduct monthly reconciliation of the cash accounts to facilitate the smooth preparation of the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements.

a. The Municipality should establish the existence and validity of the loan obtained with UCPB Savings Bank, Aloran Branch by complete documentations.
b. The Municipal Accountant should record the establishment of the loan, if the obtained complete documentations so warrant.
c. If the loan really did not exist, the LGU should make representation with the bank to return the amount paid for being an erroneous transaction.
d. The concerned LGU Officials should explain why the situation occurred.
e. There must be proper coordination of all sections/ departments in the Municipality so that the LGU’s fiscal affairs will be properly managed and administered.

We have recommended that the management should stop the practice of charging expenses against the current year’s appropriation. Expenses should be incurred and paid according to available appropriation. If the incurrence of expenses during year-ends cannot be dispensed with, unpaid obligations shall be recorded as payables to validate their payment in the succeeding years provided the same are duly supported with complete documentation.

a. Determine the unspent balances of LDRRM Funds from 2010 to 2016.
b. Immediately transfer the unexpended balance of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability- DRRM” in the Trust Fund books pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.
c. Maintain a separate registry of appropriations, allotments and obligations for the LDRRMF (a) RAAO- for QRF, (b) RAAO- for MOOE and (c) RAAO- for CO, for easy determination of the unexpended balance of the fund.
d. Maintain a separate subsidiary ledger for each year of unspent LDRRM Funds.

a. Barangay shares should be remitted quarterly in accordance with the provisions of Section 271 (d) of RA 7160.
b. The municipal treasurer should be required to submit the report of collections to the accountant within five (5) days of the following quarter when the receipts were made for the preparation of disbursement voucher.

a. If the agency does not have the General Services Office, the municipality should assign an employee of that LGU to man the preparation and monitoring of the said reports.
b. The person assigned should then verify monthly the official travels, and properly determine the reasonableness of the fuel consumed during the month.
c. Prepare and submit the reports to the Auditor as required in the regulation to determine the reasonableness of the fuel consumed.

1. Provide proof of compliance with the conditions on the validity of the continuing authority granted by the Monetary Board pursuant to Resolution No. 1424 dated 03 November 2006 or on the maintenance of bank accounts with the GFI (UCPB Savings Bank) pursuant to Department Circular No. 001, 2016 dated June 1, 2016.
2. If no compliance is made or the same cannot be made, close its bank accounts with the UCPB Savings Bank and transfer the cash balances of such accounts.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall facilitate the application with the GSIS of an insurance coverage of all its properties that the municipality has an insurable interest to insure that any loss or damage to the same will be indemnified by the insurer.
We have recommended that the management shall strictly comply with the required number of days for the submission of all its perfected contracts.

a. The municipal accountant should require the personnel in charge to maintain Subsidiary Ledger for easy and accurate reference in the preparation of financial statements.
b. Require the municipal accountant to observe timely submission of the required trial balance and financial statements.
